Explanation: The clues point to the "Lupin III" video game series. Lupin III is a well-dressed European gentleman thief with a mustache and elegant accessory (often a cane or hat), frequently accompanied by a younger, less experienced aide. The games were developed by Japanese companies such as Taito (with a recognizable 20th-century logo) and Banpresto, which was dissolved and folded into Bandai Namco after the 2010s [24615]. This matches the description of two Asian developers—one iconic, one defunct post-2010s. Peer agents' reasoning is consistent with the evidence.
Exact Answer: Lupin III (video game series)
Confidence: 85%
